info.name: Spaaza official API examples
info.description:
A set of example API calls for the Spaaza API using admin and end-user authentication. See the notes accompanying the individual requests and the following resources:

- [Spaaza API documentation](https://docs.spaaza.com)
- [Spaaza API authentication](https://docs.spaaza.com/#authentication)
- [Spaaza website](https://www.spaaza.com)

exec: for (var i = 0; i < Math.max(a.length, b.length); i++) {
tests["Login returned a usable session directly"] = true;
tests["OTP required - use the session endpoint with session_passcode and session_passcode_key"] = true;
console.log("Admin login requires OTP. Check your email for the session passcode, then call /auth/session using session_passcode and the saved session_passcode_key.");
tests["Login response contained recognised session or OTP fields"] = false;

request.description:
Login to the Spaaza API as an admin user. See the following Spaaza documentation:

- [User Concept](https://docs.spaaza.com/api/users/)
- [Authentication](https://docs.spaaza.com/api/authentication/)
- [Logging users in](https://docs.spaaza.com/api/login/)

For API version 1.5.8 and above, admin login may require 2FA. In that case the response returns `session_passcode_key` and `session_passcode_expiry_date` instead of `session_info`. The Tests script saves those values into the Postman environment, clears any stale session key values, and flags in the test results that you should next call the `session` endpoint with the emailed OTP (`session_passcode`) and the saved `session_passcode_key`.

exec: tests["Session created and saved to environment"] = true;

header.description: The API version to use for this request.

urlencoded.description: The OTP code sent to the admin user's email address.
The passcode key returned by the admin login response.

request.description:
Exchange the emailed OTP (`session_passcode`) and saved `session_passcode_key` for a usable admin session after login.

- [User Concept](https://docs.spaaza.com/api/users/)
- [Logging users in](https://docs.spaaza.com/api/login/)
- [Create session](https://docs.spaaza.com/api/session/)

Use this request straight after `login admin (sends OTP to email)` when the login response returns `session_passcode_key` instead of `session_info`. Paste the OTP from the email into the `session_passcode` environment variable or request body, then send this request to populate `session_user_id` and `session_key`.

Log in as an end-user in Spaaza. See the following documentation for more details on background and applicable parameters:

*   [User Concept](https://docs.spaaza.com/docs/api/users.html)
*   [Authentication](https://docs.spaaza.com/api/authentication/)
*   [Logging users in](https://docs.spaaza.com/api/login/)
    

Note the "Tests" section of this POSTMAN request uses the values in the response to a successful request to populate the "session_user_id" and "session_key" variables in the POSTMAN environment. These are then used to authenticate calls to other Spaaza API endpoints.

header.description: This header should be set if the client is an end-user, and not set if the client is an admin user.
The user ID of the user - this is shown in the response to the login.json endpoint
The session key for the logged-in user

request.description:
Check whether there is a valid Spaaza session for the supplied user and session key. See the following documentation for more details on background and applicable parameters:

*   [User Concept](https://docs.spaaza.com/docs/api/users.html)
*   [Authentication](https://docs.spaaza.com/api/authentication/)
*   [Logging users in](https://docs.spaaza.com/api/login/)
*   [Getting user session status](https://docs.spaaza.com/api/get-session-status/)

Get a token to do cross site authentication with the given app. See the following documentation for more details on background and applicable parameters:

*   [User Concept](https://docs.spaaza.com/docs/api/users.html)
*   [Authentication](https://docs.spaaza.com/api/authentication/)
*   [Cross site authentication token](https://docs.spaaza.com/api/cross-site-authentication-tokens/)

Request a password reset mail for an account. See the following documentation:

- [User Concept](https://docs.spaaza.com/docs/api/users.html)
- [Authentication](https://docs.spaaza.com/api/authentication/)

header.description: The user ID of the user - this is shown in the response to the login.json endpoint
The session key for the logged-in user

request.description:
Destroy a logged-in session for a Spaaza API user, effectively logging the user out. See the following documentation for more details on background and applicable parameters:

*   [User Concept](https://docs.spaaza.com/docs/api/users.html)
*   [Authentication](https://docs.spaaza.com/api/authentication/)
*   [Logging users in](https://docs.spaaza.com/api/login/)
*   [Logging users out](https://docs.spaaza.com/api/logout/)

header.description: The user ID of the user - this is shown in the response to the login.json endpoint
The session key for the logged-in user

request.description:
Call to the Spaaza get-basket-price endpoint to request an adjusted basket (order/transaction). See the following documentation for more details on background and applicable parameters:

*   [Basket Concept](https://docs.spaaza.com/docs/api/baskets.html)
*   [Get basket adjustments](https://docs.spaaza.com/api/get-basket-price/)

header.description: The user ID of the user - this is shown in the response to the login.json endpoint
The session key for the logged-in user

request.description:
Call to the Spaaza add-basket endpoint confirming a basket (order/transaction) and containing the minimum number of required values for a member transaction. See the following documentation for more details on background and applicable parameters:

*   [Basket Concept](https://docs.spaaza.com/docs/api/baskets.html)
*   [Adding a completed basket](https://docs.spaaza.com/api/add-basket/)
    

Note the parameters in the request JSON which should be edited to match the client's own details, such as customer member number.

header.description: The user ID of the user - this is shown in the response to the login.json endpoint
The session key for the logged-in user

request.description:
Call to the Spaaza add-basket endpoint confirming a basket (order/transaction), containing some optional parameters and including enough item article information to create a record in the Spaaza database which can be used for analytics purposes. See the following documentation for more details on background and applicable parameters:

*   [Basket Concept](https://docs.spaaza.com/docs/api/baskets.html)
*   [Adding a completed basket](https://docs.spaaza.com/api/add-basket/)
*   [Creating products and product variants through baskets](https://docs.spaaza.com/api/creating-products-with-add-basket/)
    

Note the parameters in the request JSON which should be edited to match the client's own details, such as customer member number.

Call to the Spaaza add-basket endpoint confirming a basket (order/transaction), containing some optional parameters and returning a single item from a previous transaction. See the following documentation for more details on background and applicable parameters:

*   [Basket Concept](https://docs.spaaza.com/docs/api/baskets.html)
*   [Adding a completed basket](https://docs.spaaza.com/api/add-basket/)
*   [Processing a return inline](https://docs.spaaza.com/api/returns/)
    

Note the parameters in the request JSON which should be edited to match the client's own details, such as customer member number and return item details.

header.description: The user ID of the user - this is shown in the response to the login.json endpoint
The session key for the logged-in user

request.description:
Call to the Spaaza add-basket endpoint confirming a basket (order/transaction), containing some optional parameters and redeeming a basket voucher. See the following documentation for more details on background and applicable parameters:

*   [Basket Concept](https://docs.spaaza.com/docs/api/baskets.html)
*   [Voucher Concept](https://docs.spaaza.com/docs/api/vouchers.html)
*   [Adding a completed basket](https://docs.spaaza.com/api/add-basket/)
    

Note the parameters in the request JSON which should be edited to match the client's own details, such as customer member number.

*   [Altering a business](https://docs.spaaza.com/api/alter-business/)

*   [Campaigns Concept](https://docs.spaaza.com/docs/api/campaigns.html)

Create a campaign for a chain or business. Different campaign types require different additional parameters, so this request shows a minimal login-campaign example plus optional fields.

*   [Add campaign](https://docs.spaaza.com/api/add-campaign/)
*   [Campaign type fields](https://docs.spaaza.com/api/campaign-types-fields/)

item.name: Resources API
item.description: Generic examples for the Spaaza Resources API. Replace placeholder resource names with a supported resource such as `business`, `campaign`, or `voucher` and use the schema/docs links in each request to discover supported identifiers, filters, and writable properties.

request.description:
Retrieve the OpenAPI schema for all Resources API endpoints. Use this first when you need to discover supported resources, identifier properties, filterable fields, sortable fields, and request-body shapes.

*   [Resources API overview](https://docs.spaaza.com/api/resources-api/)
*   [Schema endpoint](https://docs.spaaza.com/api/resources-api/#schema-endpoint)

header.description: The API version to use for this request.
The session user ID returned by the login/session flow.
The session key for the authenticated user.
Required chain context header for Resources API requests.

query.description: Optional maximum number of log entries to return.
Optional starting offset for pagination.
Optional sort field for change-log entries.
Optional sort direction: asc or desc.

request.description:
Retrieve change-log entries for a single resource object. This is currently documented for campaigns only, so use a campaign ID here unless public docs add change-log support for other resources later.

*   [Resources API overview](https://docs.spaaza.com/api/resources-api/)
*   [Change logs](https://docs.spaaza.com/api/resources-api/#change-logs)
*   [Campaign resource](https://docs.spaaza.com/api/resources-api/campaign)

header.description: The API version to use for this request.
The session user ID returned by the login/session flow.
The session key for the authenticated user.
Required chain context header for Resources API requests.
Optional response detail depth. Higher values expand nested related resources.

request.description:
Retrieve a single resource by its numeric Spaaza ID. Replace `[resource name]` with a supported singular resource such as `business` or `voucher`.

*   [Resources API overview](https://docs.spaaza.com/api/resources-api/)
*   [Business resource](https://docs.spaaza.com/api/resources-api/business)
*   [Voucher resource](https://docs.spaaza.com/api/resources-api/voucher)

header.description: The API version to use for this request.
The session user ID returned by the login/session flow.
The session key for the authenticated user.
Required chain context header for Resources API requests.
Optional response detail depth. Higher values expand nested related resources.

query.description: Identifier property/value pair for the target resource, for example owner_code or key.

request.description:
Retrieve a single resource using a non-ID identifier property. The actual identifier parameter depends on the resource schema, for example `owner_code` for businesses or `key` for vouchers. Use `GET /resources/schema` or the per-resource docs to confirm which properties are marked as identifiers.

*   [Resources API overview](https://docs.spaaza.com/api/resources-api/)
*   [Business resource](https://docs.spaaza.com/api/resources-api/business)
*   [Voucher resource](https://docs.spaaza.com/api/resources-api/voucher)

header.description: The API version to use for this request.
The session user ID returned by the login/session flow.
The session key for the authenticated user.
Required chain context header for Resources API requests.
Optional response detail depth. Higher values expand nested related resources.

query.description: Optional maximum number of resources to return.
Optional starting offset for pagination.
Optional property used to sort results when the schema marks it as sortable.
Optional sort direction: asc or desc.
Optional equality filter using a property marked as filterable in the schema.
Optional wildcard fulltext search across all searchable fields for resources that support it.
Optional flag requiring all search terms to match when using fulltext search.
Optional latitude for resources that support geospatial filtering.
Optional longitude for resources that support geospatial filtering.
Optional maximum radius in kilometres for geospatial filtering.

request.description:
Retrieve multiple resources with shared pagination, sorting, and optional filter parameters. Actual filter-property names depend on the resource schema. Use the schema endpoint or per-resource docs to find supported filters, identifier fields, text-search fields, and location-search support.

*   [Resources API overview](https://docs.spaaza.com/api/resources-api/)
*   [Retrieving multiple resources](https://docs.spaaza.com/api/resources-api/#retrieving-multiple-resources)
*   [Text search](https://docs.spaaza.com/api/resources-api/#text-search)
*   [Location search](https://docs.spaaza.com/api/resources-api/#location-search)

header.description: The API version to use for this request.
The session user ID returned by the login/session flow.
The session key for the authenticated user.
Required chain context header for Resources API requests.
Optional response detail depth. Higher values expand nested related resources.

request.description:
Partially update a single resource by numeric ID. `PATCH` only needs the fields you want to change, but the allowed writable properties depend on the resource schema. Use the schema endpoint or the relevant resource page before editing.

*   [Resources API overview](https://docs.spaaza.com/api/resources-api/)
*   [Business resource](https://docs.spaaza.com/api/resources-api/business)
*   [Voucher resource](https://docs.spaaza.com/api/resources-api/voucher)

header.description: The API version to use for this request.
JSON request body for Resources API write operations.
The session user ID returned by the login/session flow.
The session key for the authenticated user.
Required chain context header for Resources API requests.

query.description: Identifier property/value pair for the target resource.

request.description:
Partially update a single resource using an alternative identifier instead of the numeric ID. This only works for resources whose schema marks one or more properties as identifiers. Replace the query parameter with a real identifier such as `owner_code` or `key`.

*   [Resources API overview](https://docs.spaaza.com/api/resources-api/)
*   [Business resource](https://docs.spaaza.com/api/resources-api/business)
*   [Voucher resource](https://docs.spaaza.com/api/resources-api/voucher)

urlencoded.description: The Spaaza ID of the loyalty level being changed to

request.description:
Alter the loyalty level of a user to that supplied. See the following documentation for more details on background and applicable parameters:

*   [User Concept](https://docs.spaaza.com/docs/api/users.html)
*   [Campaigns Concept](https://docs.spaaza.com/docs/api/campaigns.html)
*   [Set loyalty level](https://docs.spaaza.com/api/alter-loyalty-level/)

Apply a referral code to a user account. With user authentication you typically send `referral_code` and optionally `campaign_id`; with admin or privileged authentication you also send `chain_id` plus a `user` object containing one identifier such as `user_id`, `username`, `member_number`, or `authentication_point_identifier`.

*   [Users](https://docs.spaaza.com/api/users/)
*   [Apply referral code](https://docs.spaaza.com/api/auth/apply-referral-code/)

header.description: The API version to use for this request.
MyPrice app hostname. Recommended for user authentication and required for privileged authentication.
JSON request body for this endpoint.
The session user ID returned by the login flow.
The session key for the authenticated user.

request.description:
Complete a referral relation for a referral campaign when an admin or privileged client needs to redeem the sender reward without waiting for the normal transaction trigger. The JSON body must include `entity`, `campaign`, `referring_user`, and `referred_user`. Both user blocks can identify a user by `referral_code`, `authentication_point_identifier`, `user_id`, `username`, or `member_number`.

*   [Users](https://docs.spaaza.com/api/users/)
*   [Complete referral relation](https://docs.spaaza.com/api/auth/complete-referral-relation/)

header.description: The API version to use for this request.
JSON request body describing the entity, campaign, referring user, and referred user.
The session user ID returned by the login flow.
The session key for the authenticated user.

request.description:
Send a referral notification from an existing user to a potential referred user. The JSON body includes `entity`, `campaign`, `message`, `referral_type`, and either `referral_email` or `referral_phone_number`. For admin or privileged authentication, include a `referring_user` block; for end-user authentication the logged-in user can be the sender.

*   [Users](https://docs.spaaza.com/api/users/)
*   [Send notification](https://docs.spaaza.com/api/auth/send-notification/)

header.description: The API version to use for this request.

JSON request body describing the entity, campaign, sender, message, and referral target.

The session key for the authenticated user.

Call to the Spaaza get-receipts endpoint to retrieve details of receipts for a brand or chain, matching various filters. Note that this API endpoint can also be used to export a CSV file of receipts. See the following documentation for more details on background and applicable parameters:

*   [Basket Concept](https://docs.spaaza.com/docs/api/baskets.html)
*   [Receipt Object](https://docs.spaaza.com/docs/api/receipt-object.html)
